text: HTML5 (2014) introduced semantic elements that describe their meaning to both browser and developer: `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<article>`, `<section>`, `<aside>`, `<footer>`, `<figure>`, `<time>`. They improve accessibility (screen readers), SEO, and code readability.

HTML5 (2014) introduced semantic elements that describe their meaning to both browser and developer: `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<article>`, `<section>`, `<aside>`, `<footer>`, `<figure>`, `<time>`. They improve accessibility (screen readers), SEO, and code readability.
